title: Exercises: Degradable Channels
description: Practice analyzing degradable and anti-degradable channels

exercises.title: Complementary Channel Computation
exercises.description: Implement the complementary channel for amplitude damping and verify the degradability transition at gamma = 0.5.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def complementary_ad(rho, gamma):
    """Compute complementary channel output for amplitude damping."""
    pass

def check_degradability(gamma):
    """Check if AD is degradable at given gamma."""
    pass
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def von_neumann_entropy(rho):
    eigvals = np.linalg.eigvalsh(rho)
    eigvals = eigvals[eigvals > 1e-15]
    return -np.sum(eigvals * np.log2(eigvals)) if len(eigvals) > 0 else 0

def amplitude_damping(rho, gamma):
    K0 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1-gamma)]])
    K1 = np.array([[0, np.sqrt(gamma)], [0, 0]])
    return K0 @ rho @ K0.conj().T + K1 @ rho @ K1.conj().T

def complementary_ad(rho, gamma):
    """Complementary channel for AD."""
    # Kraus operators
    K0 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1-gamma)]])
    K1 = np.array([[0, np.sqrt(gamma)], [0, 0]])
    kraus = [K0, K1]
    
    # Build environment state
    n_kraus = len(kraus)
    rho_E = np.zeros((n_kraus, n_kraus), dtype=complex)
    for j, Kj in enumerate(kraus):
        for k, Kk in enumerate(kraus):
            rho_E[j, k] = np.trace(Kj.conj().T @ Kk @ rho)
    return rho_E

def check_degradability(gamma, num_samples=50):
    """Check degradability by comparing S(B) vs S(E)."""
    # For degradable: S(E) <= S(B) for all inputs
    # For anti-degradable: S(B) <= S(E) for all inputs
    
    S_B_list = []
    S_E_list = []
    
    for theta in np.linspace(0, np.pi/2, num_samples):
        psi = np.array([np.cos(theta), np.sin(theta)])
        rho = np.outer(psi, psi.conj())
        
        rho_B = amplitude_damping(rho, gamma)
        rho_E = complementary_ad(rho, gamma)
        
        S_B_list.append(von_neumann_entropy(rho_B))
        S_E_list.append(von_neumann_entropy(rho_E))
    
    # Check conditions
    degradable = all(se <= sb + 1e-10 for sb, se in zip(S_B_list, S_E_list))
    anti_degradable = all(sb <= se + 1e-10 for sb, se in zip(S_B_list, S_E_list))
    
    return {
        'gamma': gamma,
        'degradable': degradable,
        'anti_degradable': anti_degradable,
        'max_I_c': max(sb - se for sb, se in zip(S_B_list, S_E_list))
    }

# Test degradability transition
print("Degradability Analysis for Amplitude Damping:")
print("-" * 50)
for gamma in [0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9]:
    result = check_degradability(gamma)
    status = "Degradable" if result['degradable'] else ("Anti-degradable" if result['anti_degradable'] else "Neither")
    print(f"gamma={gamma}: {status}, max I_c = {result['max_I_c']:.4f}")
hints: Complementary channel: rho_E[j,k] = Tr[Kj^dag Kk rho]
Degradable iff S(E) <= S(B) for all inputs

exercises.title: Degradable Channel Capacity
exercises.description: Compute the quantum capacity of degradable amplitude damping using the single-letter formula.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np
from scipy.optimize import minimize_scalar

def quantum_capacity_degradable(gamma):
    """Compute Q for degradable AD (gamma <= 0.5)."""
    pass
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np
from scipy.optimize import minimize_scalar

def von_neumann_entropy(rho):
    eigvals = np.linalg.eigvalsh(rho)
    eigvals = eigvals[eigvals > 1e-15]
    return -np.sum(eigvals * np.log2(eigvals)) if len(eigvals) > 0 else 0

def amplitude_damping(rho, gamma):
    K0 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1-gamma)]])
    K1 = np.array([[0, np.sqrt(gamma)], [0, 0]])
    return K0 @ rho @ K0.conj().T + K1 @ rho @ K1.conj().T

def complementary_ad(rho, gamma):
    K0 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1-gamma)]])
    K1 = np.array([[0, np.sqrt(gamma)], [0, 0]])
    kraus = [K0, K1]
    n_kraus = len(kraus)
    rho_E = np.zeros((n_kraus, n_kraus), dtype=complex)
    for j, Kj in enumerate(kraus):
        for k, Kk in enumerate(kraus):
            rho_E[j, k] = np.trace(Kj.conj().T @ Kk @ rho)
    return rho_E

def coherent_info(theta, gamma):
    """Coherent information for input state with parameter theta."""
    psi = np.array([np.cos(theta), np.sin(theta)])
    rho = np.outer(psi, psi.conj())
    
    rho_B = amplitude_damping(rho, gamma)
    rho_E = complementary_ad(rho, gamma)
    
    S_B = von_neumann_entropy(rho_B)
    S_E = von_neumann_entropy(rho_E)
    
    return S_B - S_E

def quantum_capacity_degradable(gamma):
    """Q = max I_c for degradable AD."""
    if gamma > 0.5:
        return 0  # Anti-degradable
    
    result = minimize_scalar(
        lambda t: -coherent_info(t, gamma),
        bounds=(0, np.pi/2),
        method='bounded'
    )
    return max(0, -result.fun)

# Compute capacity curve
print("Quantum Capacity of Amplitude Damping:")
print("-" * 40)
gammas = np.linspace(0, 0.6, 13)
for gamma in gammas:
    Q = quantum_capacity_degradable(gamma)
    bar = "*" * int(Q * 20)
    print(f"gamma={gamma:.2f}: Q={Q:.4f} {bar}")

print("\nNote: Q=0 for gamma >= 0.5 (anti-degradable)")

exercises.title: Degrading Map Construction
exercises.description: For the erasure channel, explicitly construct the degrading map that shows it is degradable.
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def erasure_channel(rho, p, dim=2):
    """Erasure channel: output is rho or |e> with prob p.
    Output space: (dim+1) dimensional with |e> as erasure flag.
    """
    output = np.zeros((dim+1, dim+1), dtype=complex)
    output[:dim, :dim] = (1-p) * rho
    output[dim, dim] = p  # Erasure flag
    return output

def erasure_complement(rho, p, dim=2):
    """Complementary channel for erasure.
    Environment learns: erasure happened or not.
    """
    # Environment is 2-dim: |0> = no erasure, |1> = erasure
    rho_E = np.zeros((2, 2), dtype=complex)
    rho_E[0, 0] = 1 - p  # No erasure
    rho_E[1, 1] = p      # Erasure
    return rho_E

def degrading_map(rho_B, p, dim=2):
    """Degrading map D such that N^c = D o N.
    
    If we receive the state (in first dim x dim block): output |0><0|
    If we receive erasure (in (dim,dim) position): output |1><1|
    """
    # Extract what was received
    state_part = rho_B[:dim, :dim]
    erasure_prob = np.real(rho_B[dim, dim])
    
    # Degrading map output
    rho_E = np.zeros((2, 2), dtype=complex)
    rho_E[0, 0] = np.trace(state_part)  # Prob of receiving state
    rho_E[1, 1] = erasure_prob          # Prob of erasure
    
    return rho_E

# Verify degradability: N^c = D o N
np.random.seed(42)
for p in [0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7]:
    # Random input state
    psi = np.random.randn(2) + 1j * np.random.randn(2)
    psi /= np.linalg.norm(psi)
    rho = np.outer(psi, psi.conj())
    
    # Direct complementary
    rho_E_direct = erasure_complement(rho, p)
    
    # Via degrading: D o N
    rho_B = erasure_channel(rho, p)
    rho_E_degraded = degrading_map(rho_B, p)
    
    match = np.allclose(rho_E_direct, rho_E_degraded)
    print(f"p={p}: N^c = D o N? {match}")

print("\nErasure channel is degradable for all p!")
hints: Degrading map extracts whether erasure occurred
